message:
fuzz: add fuzz_snapshot_roundtrip target with node_attrs invariants

New structure-aware fuzz target exercising the snapshot → replay
round-trip across Graph / DiGraph / MultiGraph / MultiDiGraph.
The recent fnx-classes audit added a
``node_attrs: BTreeMap<String, AttrMap>`` field to all four
snapshot types; this target gives that fix continuous coverage.

Beyond no-panic, asserts:

Determinism
  - graph.snapshot() == graph.snapshot() (same graph snapshotted
    twice produces equal snapshot structs)

Replay invariants
  - Replayed graph has the same node_count, edge_count, and
    has_edge-set as the original.
  - Per-node attribute preservation: for every key in the
    original snapshot's node_attrs map, the replayed graph
    reports the same AttrMap.
  - Re-snapshotting the replayed graph yields the original
    snapshot exactly (full structural identity, including
    attribute maps).

Revision monotonicity
  - graph.revision() must NOT change across read-only snapshot
    calls.

Variants
  - 4 round-trip variants (Graph / DiGraph / MultiGraph /
    MultiDiGraph)
  - 2 determinism variants
  - 1 revision-stability variant
  - 2 with-synthesized-node-attrs variants that sprinkle
    fnx_color / fnx_weight / fnx_count attrs onto half the
    nodes before snapshotting, exercising the node_attrs path

Includes a coverage-minimized seed corpus of 383 inputs
harvested from a 60s libfuzzer pass + merge=1 minimization
(2603 cov edges, 10389 features).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+name = "fuzz_snapshot_roundtrip"
+path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_snapshot_roundtrip.rs"
+doc = false
